title: DataTools4Heart Clinical Entity Extraction Qualitative Evaluation Protocol
author: Lima López, Salvador
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Description  
 This record provides a structured evaluation package for the qualitative assessment of clinical Named Entity Recognition (NER) systems applied to cardiology-related text. The materials are designed to support expert review of system-generated annotations and to complement standard quantitative evaluation metrics with clinician-oriented evidence on output quality, error patterns, usability and potential deployment in hospital NLP workflows. 
 The package includes an evaluator instruction document and two parallel questionnaires. The first questionnaire is intended for the assessment of a baseline NER system based on rules and dictionary matching. The second questionnaire is intended for the assessment of a Transformers-based NER system. Both questionnaires follow the same structure, enabling a direct qualitative comparison between the two approaches. 
 The evaluation focuses on four clinically relevant entity types frequently required for the structuring of cardiology narratives: diseases, symptoms, procedures and medications. Evaluators are asked to assess the general quality of the extracted entities, provide entity-specific ratings, indicate the perceived usefulness of automatic entity extraction, and report observed error types. The error taxonomy covers boundary and span errors, label misclassification, missing entities, false positives, ambiguity and context-related errors, tokenisation issues and domain-specific terminology errors. Free-text fields are also included to capture expert observations that may not be reflected by closed questions. 
 These materials are intended to provide a reproducible framework for qualitative evaluation of clinical information extraction systems. They are particularly relevant in settings where automatic benchmark metrics need to be complemented with expert judgement on clinical interpretability, practical usefulness and readiness for integration into real-world data processing pipelines. Although developed for cardiology-oriented NER, the structure of the evaluation package can be adapted to other clinical domains, entity schemas or multilingual NLP evaluation scenarios. 
  Funding  
 This work was funded by the European project DataTools4Heart (Grant Agreement No. 101057849). Fernando Gallego-Donoso and Salvador Lima-López fellowship within the “Generación D” initiative,  Red.es , Ministerio para la Transformación Digital y de la Función Pública, for talent attraction (C005/24-ED CV1). Funded by the European Union NextGenerationEU funds, through PRTR.
